Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class ExternalDataSource

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.ExternalDataSource
public class ExternalDataSource extends Metadata

Java class for ExternalDataSource complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="ExternalDataSource">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="authProvider" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="certificate" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="customConfiguration" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="customHttpHeaders" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}CustomHttpHeader" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="endpoint" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="externalDataSrcDescriptors" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ExternalDataSrcDescriptor" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="isWritable" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="label" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="namedCredential" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="oauthRefreshToken" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="oauthScope" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="oauthToken" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="password" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="principalType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ExternalPrincipalType"/>
         <element name="protocol" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}AuthenticationProtocol"/>
         <element name="repository" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="type" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}ExternalDataSourceType"/>
         <element name="username" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="version" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getAuthProvider

      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

      possible object is String

      Gets the value of the customConfiguration property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • setCustomConfiguration

      public void setCustomConfiguration(String value)
      Sets the value of the customConfiguration property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getCustomHttpHeaders

      public List<CustomHttpHeader> getCustomHttpHeaders()
      Gets the value of the customHttpHeaders property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the customHttpHeaders property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getCustomHttpHeaders().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list CustomHttpHeader

      Returns:
      The value of the customHttpHeaders property.

    • getEndpoint

      public String getEndpoint()
      Gets the value of the endpoint property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • setEndpoint

      public void setEndpoint(String value)
      Sets the value of the endpoint property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getExternalDataSrcDescriptors

      public List<ExternalDataSrcDescriptor> getExternalDataSrcDescriptors()
      Gets the value of the externalDataSrcDescriptors property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the externalDataSrcDescriptors property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getExternalDataSrcDescriptors().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list ExternalDataSrcDescriptor

      Returns:
      The value of the externalDataSrcDescriptors property.
